rulings.Case Title: CAPITAL ONE BANK VS. BERNADETT A GAERLAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Hearing On Claim Of Exemption; Opposition
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 1, 2013, Line 16. Hearing On Claim Of Exemption. Judgment Debtor Gaerlan's claim of exemption is denied. Plaintiff has not carried her burden of showing that all earnings are exempt. (CCP § 703.580.) The Court orders the Los Angeles County Sherriff's Department to release the levied funds to satisfy the debt. Twenty five dollars are ordered to be withheld from Debtor's earnings per pay period until the debt is satisfied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. PROPERTY SUBJECT TO DISPOSITION: $2150.00 U.S. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order For Terminating Sanctions And Striking Claim
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 1, 2013, Line 2, PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA's Motion For Order For Terminating Sanctions And Striking Claim. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. It would be helpful to the court if the People were prepared to address the following issue: From the Declaration of Assistant District Attorney Jeffrey Ross it appears that, at the time of service of the Court's prior order granting the People's motion to compel discovery responses, Claimant Rafael Cabrera was no longer residing at the address listed on his claim where he was served with the notice of entry of order. Does the District Attorney's office know if Mr. Cabrera is currently incarcerated as a result of any charges that may have been brought against him in connection with his July 4, 2008 arrest? If so, can the District Attorney's office serve him where he is incarcerated with notice of the motion for terminating sanctions? =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: TBF FINANCIAL, LLC, A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY VS. IMMUNE ENHANCEMENT PROJECT, A CORPORATION et al

rulings.Case Title: TBF FINANCIAL, LLC, A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY VS. IMMUNE ENHANCEMENT PROJECT, A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 1, 2013, Line 17. PLAINTIFF TBF FINANCIAL, LLC'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Off calendar. At the request of the parties, the Court continued the hearing date on this motion--originally set for June 18, 2013--to September 25, 2013. The hearing was continued again on September 25, 2013 to October 1, 2013, per agreement of the parties. Rather than simply continuing the hearing date per the first request made by the parties, Plaintiff filed an amended notice of motion, amended memorandum of points and authorities, amended and supplemental declarations, and an amended separate statement on June 14, 2013. The amended notice of motion, filed June 14, 2013, states that the motion relies on "this notice, all of the pleadings, records and papers previously served filed herein, the supplemental declaration, the amended points and authorities and amended separate statement attached hereto." Further, the Declaration of Lorna Walker in Support of Motion for Summary Judgment Pursuant to CCP 1008 was not filed until June 14, 2013-four days before the original hearing date. In effect, Plaintiff has filed a third motion for summary judgment, and without making clear to the Court the specific pleadings, records and papers upon which the current motion relies. The Court cannot determine what papers, if any, are superseded by the "amended motion" filed on June 14, 2013. The motion is thus taken off calendar. Further, the Court notes that Defendant's Response to Plaintiff's Amended Separate Statement fails to comply with Rule of Court 3.1350(f), which requires that "each material fact claimed by the moving party to be undisputed must set out verbatim on the left side of the page, below which must be set out the evidence said by the moving party to establish that fact, complete with the moving party's references to exhibits." (emphasis added) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ERNICE FAGAN VS. SAN FRANCISCO GENERAL HOSPITAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion O Vacate Dismissal Of Action
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 1, 2013, Line 2. PLAINTIFF ERNICE FAGAN'S Motion to Vacate Dismissal Of Action. Hearing required. The Court is inclined to rule as follows: Plaintiff's motion to set aside dismissal is denied as untimely. Plaintiff states that she voluntarily dismissed this case (516891) ("Case 1") on June 26, 2012, "in anticipation of the hearing that was set for the demurrer" on her Second Amended Cross Complaint that was then scheduled to be heard on June 27, 2012. (Memo. at 1.) Plaintiff then filed another case (525904) ("Case 2") on November 7, 2012 regarding the same incident that is the subject of Case 1. Plaintiff did not file the present motion to set aside her voluntary dismissal in Case 1 until June 20, 2013. This is not within six months of the dismissal that was entered on June 26, 2012, and which is required by statute. (CCP § 473(b).) =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT CARTER et al VS. PACIFIC UNION REAL ESTATE GROUP LTD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Seal Records
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 1, 2013, Line 5. DEFENDANT PACIFIC UNION INTERNATIONAL, INC.'S Motion To Seal Records. Granted in part; denied without prejudice in part. Moving Party Pacific Union International, Inc.'s (PUI) motion to seal certain records filed in connection with previous discovery motions (Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Compliance With Subpoena filed August 13, 2013 and Plaintiffs' Motion to Compel Compliance With Subpoena filed August 20, 2013) is granted. The requirements of Rules 2.550-2.551 do not apply to discovery motions. (Rule 2.550(a)(3).) A specific order must be prepared by moving party identifying the documents that are to be filed under seal in connection with these discovery motions. The remainder of the motion is denied without prejudice. The moving party has not met its burden to seal any or all of these documents. Without further showing, including a copy of the specific documents which are the subject of the sealing request with the specific portions sought to be sealed identified for the Court to review, the Court cannot make the express factual findings required under Rule 2.550(d). Further, the records have already been filed in the public record. Any further motion to seal requires a representation from counsel that it did not receive timely notice that confidential documents were being lodged with the Court, as required by Rule 2.551(b)(3)(iii). The Court cannot tell from the moving papers whether the documents at issue were identified as confidential, and whether the requirements of Rule 2.551(b)(3)(iii) were met. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: SIDNEY CLAYTON HOLTER VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Disclose Personnel Records
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 1, 2013, Line 3, PLAINTIFF SIDNEY HOLTER's Motion To Disclose Personnel Records. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The court will bifurcate the hearing. For the first portion of the hearing, it would be helpful to the court for the parties to be prepared to address their respective positions on the threshold question of whether the declarations submitted by Plaintiff establish good cause for the discovery sought under Evidence Code section 1043, subdivision (b)(3) by providing a specific factual scenario establishing a plausible factual foundation for the allegations of misconduct. If the threshold cannot be met, than the discovery sought is not warranted. If at the conclusion of the first part of the hearing, the court determines that the threshold has been met, in the second part of the hearing the court will conduct an in-camera review of the materials sought in order to determine those portions of the records that will be produced. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CONSUMER ADVOCACY GROUP, INC., IN THE PUBLIC VS. KOHL'S CORPORATION, A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For An Order Compelling Further Responses To Pltff'S First Set Of Requests For Admission To Def Kohl'S Department Stores, Inc. And Imposing Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 1, 2013, Line 4, PLAINTIFF CONSUMER ADVOCACY GROUP, INC., IN THE PUBLIC INTEREST's Motion For An Order Compelling Further Responses To Plaintiff'S First Set Of Requests For Admission To Defendant Kohl'S Department Stores, Inc. And Imposing Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED. Defendant Kohl's Department Stores, Inc.'s responses to Plaintiff's Requests for Admission Nos. 7, 13, 14 and 15 admit the requests for admission for only a portion of the time period stated in the request, without denying or providing a statement of inability to comply with respect to the remaining time period specified in the requests for admission. Requests for Admissions 71 and 72 call for the application of law to fact, and are therefore proper. Plaintiff's request for monetary sanctions is DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For An Order Compelling Further Responses To Pltff'S First Set Of Requests For Production To Def Kohl'S Department Stores, Inc. And Imposing Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 1, 2013, Line 4, PLAINTIFF CONSUMER ADVOCACY GROUP, INC., IN THE PUBLIC INTEREST's Motion For An Order Compelling Further Responses To Plaintiff'S First Set Of Requests For Production To Defendant Kohl'S Department Stores, Inc. And Imposing Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: HEARING REQUIRED. The requests for production seek information relevant to the subject matter involved in the pending action. Defendant has responded to the Requests for Production with pure objections, including overbreadth objections, and without offering to produce any documents at all in response to the requests. The parties shall meet and confer prior to the hearing to attempt to agree on the scope of documents to be produced in response to each of the requests, and shall report to the court any remaining areas of disagreement. At the hearing, it would also be helpful to the court for the parties to be prepared to explain their positions concerning the question of whether Defendant's refusal to produce a single document in response to Plaintiff's requests for production constitutes acting with substantial justification within the meaning of Code of Civil Procedure Section 2033.290 subdivision (d). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ERIK SWENSON et al VS. ROMAN SHADE COMPANY OF SAN MATEO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 1, 2013, Line 6, DEFENDANT MARGARET MINTO's Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. Defendant Margaret Minto's motion to compel is GRANTED with respect to Special Interrogatories 1, 15, 16, 17 and 18. Special Interrogatories 2, 3, 4, 13, and 14 seek information that is not relevant to the subject matter involved in the pending action or to the determination of any motion made in the action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICK AARON VS. TRINITY MANAGEMENT SERVICES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Discovery Responses And For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $6120.00
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 1, 2013, Line 7, DEFENDANT TRINITY MANAGEMENT SERVICES, INC.'s Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $6120.00. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED. Plaintiff's cell phone records are relevant to the subject matter involved in the pending action, and are in his possession, custody, or control, despite the fact that the cell phone account may be owned by his mother in law. Plaintiff has the right to obtain from the cell phone carrier the records that pertain to his cell phone number. Plaintiff may redact his mother-in-law's mailing address and contact information if he so chooses. Plaintiff's Special Interrogatories Nos. 9, 10, and 11, seek information that is relevant to Plaintiff's mitigation efforts. Within 30 days Plaintiff shall provide documents responsive to Defendant's Request for Production No. 22, and shall provide Defendant with verified, code-compliant responses to Defendant's Special Interrogatories Nos. 9, 10 and 11. Defendant's request for sanctions is DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PING CHING WONG et al VS. TSUNG TSIN ASSOCIATION, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 1, 2013, Line 10. DEFENDANTS EUGENE CHAN, BIANNE CHAN, NYUGET BACH DU, STEVEN YANG, EVA CHUK, LY K TAC, DAVID NGHIEM, THO HOA TRAN, NHUT SAP TSAN, LIN FU FUNG'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The Court sustains the demurrer to the negligence and negligent infliction of emotional distress causes of action with leave to amend because plaintiff failed to comply with CCP 425.15. Further, the negligence claims describe intentional misconduct, not negligence. In addition, the negligent infliction of emotional distress is not a distinct tort. The tort is negligence. See Burgess v. Sup. Crt., (1992) 2 Cal.4th 1064, 1072. The court overrules the demurrer as to the slander cause of action. The Court cannot determine by this demurrer whether Civil Code 47(c) applies because it is a conditional privilege. The Court overrules the demurrer to the intentional infliction of emotional distress cause of action because the Complaint sufficiently pleads outrageous conduct. The Court sustains without leave to amend as to the fraud cause of action because plaintiff cannot allege justifiable reliance. The Court sustains with leave to amend as to the conspiracy claims and claim for injunctive relief. The conspiracy allegations should be pleaded as a general charging allegation, and the request for injunctive relief should be alleged as a prayer for relief. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
